vulnerabilities.description: CSRF handling appears flawed: posting without csrf_token or with an empty token yields a 500 Internal Server Error instead of a CSRF rejection, and posting with an invalid token proceeds to CAPTCHA validation (403 Invalid CAPTCHA). This suggests the token is not robustly validated and error handling is improper.

A captcha cookie is issued client-side and is not HttpOnly or SameSite. Although it appears encrypted, it is readable by client-side scripts and could be exfiltrated via XSS to bypass CAPTCHA. Session cookie also lacks SameSite.

Server header discloses Werkzeug/2.2.3 and Python/3.8.20, indicating the Flask development server is used. Running a dev server in production is insecure and may expose additional risk if debug is ever enabled.
Responses lack common security headers (Content-Security-Policy, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, HSTS), increasing exposure to XSS and clickjacking.
